Strategic growth consultancy

Unlocking the commercial value already inside your business.

Finch Theory helps ambitious businesses identify and unlock the commercial value already inside them.

Most businesses are sitting on untapped growth. Existing relationships, underused networks, unrealised opportunities. The value is there. We identify it, structure it and turn it into measurable results.

The opportunity

Most businesses are sitting on more than they realise.

Growth does not always require more resources, more marketing or more people. Often the greatest opportunities already exist inside the business: relationships that have never been activated, networks that have never been structured, and team capacity being quietly eroded by pressures no one has addressed.

The question is not whether the value is there. It is whether anyone is doing anything about it.

i
Untapped Relationships Your professional network contains introductions, partnerships and commercial opportunities that have never been identified or activated. Most businesses leave this value entirely untouched.
ii
Hidden Performance Costs Financial pressure within your workforce appears as absenteeism, turnover and reduced productivity. Most businesses treat the symptoms. Few identify the underlying cause.
iii
No Commercial Infrastructure Growing businesses often lack the senior BD capability to build and convert a pipeline. Without that infrastructure, opportunities are missed and growth stalls.
Your greatest growth opportunity

The value is already inside your business.

Every organisation has spent years accumulating professional relationships. Clients, suppliers, advisers, partners, industry contacts. Most of that relationship capital sits entirely unmanaged. No structure. No strategy. No activation.

The introductions that would change your business are sitting in your existing network. The partnerships that would accelerate your growth are already within reach. The opportunities are there.

Most businesses do not have a growth problem. They have an activation problem.

Finch Theory identifies, structures and activates relationship capital to create introductions, strategic partnerships and new commercial opportunities from assets the business already holds.

Activate your relationship capital
i

Identify

Map the full landscape of existing relationships, contacts and networks. Identify where commercial value sits, dormant and unactivated.

ii

Structure

Build the frameworks and processes that turn an unmanaged network into a structured commercial asset with a clear strategy behind it.

iii

Activate

Deploy the relationship capital. Generate introductions, create strategic partnerships and convert existing contacts into new commercial opportunities.

iv

Measure

Track introductions made, partnerships formed and opportunities created. Relationship capital is a commercial asset. It should be measured like one.

Matthew Steiner, Founder

About

Matthew Steiner

Founder, Finch Theory

Over twenty years in financial services spanning financial planning, asset management, business advisory and strategic consultancy. Matthew began his career as a financial adviser before moving into asset management with a merchant bank, working on tax-advantaged investments including VCTs, EIS and inheritance tax planning.

He works directly with founders and leadership teams of growing businesses, combining technical expertise with practical delivery to create outcomes that are measurable from the outset.

Building automated digital marketing workflows since 2013, with hands-on experience of Infusionsoft and Go High Level. Finch Theory engagements can include workflow design and automation alongside advisory work where relevant.

The work is personal. At 19, Matthew was diagnosed with leukaemia and became the second person in the UK to receive a stem cell transplant. That experience shaped a particular clarity about what matters, what is worth building, and the importance of doing work that creates genuine value for others.

Ambassador for the Royal Marsden Cancer Charity in Surrey

Tenor performing at the London Marathon and Wimbledon queue

Volunteer in Nepal, teaching English and supporting orphanage work

What we do

Three ways to unlock commercial value.

Each service targets a different source of untapped value inside your business. Most clients find that more than one applies.

01

Relationship Capital

Turn your network into new revenue

Most businesses invest heavily in marketing and lead generation while overlooking the relationships they have already spent years building. Relationship capital consultancy identifies, organises and activates those assets, creating introductions, partnerships and commercial opportunities that would not otherwise exist.

Referral and introduction frameworks that generate consistent new opportunities Strategic partnerships identified and activated from your existing network New commercial opportunities from relationships you already hold
Create new opportunities

02

Workforce Performance

Recover the value financial pressure is costing you

Financial pressure within the workforce shows up as reduced productivity, absenteeism, staff turnover and disengagement. A structured approach combining benefits consultancy, employee financial education and ongoing support delivers measurable, documented improvements in performance and retention.

Reduced absenteeism and staff turnover Improved employee engagement and resilience Measurable return on employee investment
Improve team performance

03

Fractional BD

Build a commercial pipeline without a full-time hire

Acting as your senior commercial function on a retained or project basis. Pipeline strategy, opportunity qualification, pitch support and commercial partnerships. Senior BD capability, structured pipeline discipline and commercial judgment, without the cost or commitment of a permanent hire.

Structured pipeline built from first principles Senior commercial judgment on every opportunity Results measured from day one
Build your pipeline
Why Finch Theory

The name has a theory behind it.

In 1835, Charles Darwin sailed to the Galapagos Islands aboard the HMS Beagle. What he found there changed how we understand life itself.

Across the islands, he collected multiple species of finch, closely related yet strikingly different. Each had evolved a distinct beak shape, precisely suited to the food sources available on its particular island. Crushing hard seeds. Probing cactus flowers. Extracting insects from bark.

The birds had not been given different tools. They had adapted the tools they already possessed to find and unlock value in their specific environment. Darwin called it adaptive radiation.

We call it the operating principle behind every successful business.

The Finch Theory is not a philosophy. It is a practical commitment to finding the value that already exists, and building on it.

I

Adaptation

The finches did not change their environment. They found ways to extract value from it that others had missed. Every business contains latent value waiting to be unlocked by the right approach.

II

Specialisation

Each species developed precise capability for a specific opportunity. Finch Theory brings exactly the right expertise to each engagement, targeted rather than generalist.

III

Evolution

Businesses that grow are not always the strongest. They are the ones that adapt fastest. We find what works and build on it systematically.

Who we help

Built for businesses that are ready to grow.

Finch Theory works with a small number of clients at any one time. The work is senior, hands-on and commercially grounded throughout.

i

Founders and Owner-managers

You are generating revenue but growth has plateaued. You know the opportunity is there but lack the commercial infrastructure to convert it.

Finch Theory acts as your senior commercial partner, identifying where value sits and building the systems to unlock it. No permanent overhead. Results from day one.

ii

Professional Services Firms

You have deep expertise and strong client relationships. But referrals are inconsistent and your network is not being used as the commercial asset it is.

Relationship capital consultancy structures and activates the professional networks that professional services firms spend years building, converting them into consistent introductions and new instructions.

iii

SME Leadership Teams

Your team is your greatest asset, but financial pressure is affecting performance, retention and culture in ways that are hard to quantify and harder to address.

Workforce performance consultancy identifies the underlying cause and builds a structured response. The commercial return is measurable and typically exceeds the cost of the engagement within the first year.

iv

Growing Businesses

You need senior commercial thinking now but are not yet at the stage where a full-time BD director makes sense. The pipeline is not building itself.

A fractional BD engagement provides the pipeline discipline, opportunity qualification and commercial judgment of a senior hire, on a retained or project basis that scales with your business.

How we work

No cost. No proposal. Until we understand your situation.

Every engagement begins with a conversation. Nothing is recommended or costed until the business, the team and the real constraint are properly understood.

01

Conversation

A focused 30-minute call to understand your situation and whether there is a genuine fit. No obligation. You come away with a clearer picture of where the opportunity may be, whether we work together or not.

02

Discovery

A deeper session mapping your commercial infrastructure: BD activity, team dynamics and the professional relationships that currently support or limit your growth.

03

Proposal

A written proposal setting out exactly what is recommended, how it will be delivered, the expected outcomes and the cost. The first point at which any commitment is invited.

04

Delivery

Active delivery managed throughout. Progress reviewed at agreed intervals. Outcomes documented and measurable from the outset.

Working with me

For recruiters and
hiring businesses

Finch Theory takes on a small number of fractional engagements each year. Senior, hands-on relationships with businesses that want an experienced outside perspective without a permanent internal voice.

This is not a placement. Fractional engagements run on a retained or project basis, defined by outcomes rather than hours. If you are a recruiter or a business considering whether a fractional arrangement might be the right fit, the information here should help you assess that.

For interim or non-executive roles where the scope, sector and purpose are a strong match, introductions are welcome. The best route is always a direct conversation first.

Current availability

Two to three fractional engagements taken on each year. Typical duration is six to twelve months. Sector focus is SME, professional services and financial services. Write to matthew@finchtheory.com with a brief note on the context.

Fractional BD Director

Retained

Ongoing retained relationship acting as senior commercial function. Two to three days per month with structured pipeline reviews and direct client engagement. Minimum six-month commitment.

Relationship Capital Audit

Project

A defined project engagement mapping and activating relationship assets. Delivered over eight to twelve weeks with a clear output and implementation framework.

Workforce Performance Review

Project or retained

Diagnostic and structured programme addressing financial wellbeing within the workforce. Delivered directly or with specialist partners depending on scale and scope.

Non-executive or Advisory

By introduction

A small number of NED and advisory board positions considered where sector, stage and purpose align. Direct approaches also considered.
